body_txt:
According to the media, certain professions almost systematically attract people with camp sensibilities and Drama Queens . Most typically, these are "hip" professions linked to show business or the arts. Although most noticeable of male characters, it still affects women quite frequently. Bonus point if they're French or have some sort of accent , double bonus if they are Camp Gay (they tend to be at least Ambiguously Gay ), and if they say "I am not an X, I am an artist ", oh boy, do you have a winner. Such professions include:  Can be Truth in Television in some societies and/or eras for the reason that these professions are based on talent: so they tend to be tolerant of people with unusual mannerisms or personality quirks. But can also not be the case. If society in general is tolerant or even supportive of this behavior, then it's not this trope.
Common variant : a tough working-class manly-man has to act the part to get work in one of the above. Compare Real Men Wear Pink .
